headline: Ecuador first agent baseline. Freedom House 2026: Noboa 'Fénix' emergency security plan against transnational crime documented arbitrary arrests, extrajudicial killings, torture of detainees. Ecuador now among most violent Latin American countries. Global Initiative January 2026 report: criminal governance in Ecuador's prison system; 'carceral capture.' Published composite 35.9 / Developing band reflects pre-Fénix baseline; compound evidence on extrajudicial killings, torture, and prison-system criminal-governance suggests dimension pressure on BND (-0.5), ACC (-0.5), and INT (-0.5) — declared rights commitments contradicted by security-state operationalization. Composite reconstructs to ~31.3. Developing band sustained; sub-threshold under 5-point material rule but compound pattern is significant. Recommend downgrade with boundary-case approval.
boundary_note: Sub-threshold delta (-4.6 points) under the 5-point material-change rule. Confirm at 35.9 with documented dimension-pressure notation. Boundary-case approved: medium-confidence evidence consistent with downward pressure but does not yet meet 5-point threshold for material change. Re-evaluate within 60 days as Fénix plan operationalizes and prison-system reform efforts mature.
key_evidence: Freedom House 2026: Ecuador's fight against transnational crime is eroding human rights.
Noboa 'Fénix' emergency security plan: documented arbitrary arrests, extrajudicial killings, torture of detainees.
Ecuador now among most violent Latin American countries.
Global Initiative January 2026 report: criminal governance in Ecuador's prison system ('carceral capture').
January 2024 internal armed conflict declaration; sustained military deployment posture.
TC Television live-broadcast hostage incident (January 2024) and aftermath context.

evidence.finding: Fénix plan: arbitrary arrests, extrajudicial killings, detainee torture; rights erosion under security-state framing.
Criminal governance documented in Ecuador's prison system; structural state-capacity gap.

scoring_rationale.AWR: 2.5 confirmed under pressure. Civil society and Defensoría del Pueblo monitoring functional; security-context information control limits state recognition mechanisms.
scoring_rationale.ACT: 2.5 confirmed under pressure. Fénix plan represents action mobilization but with documented harm pattern (extrajudicial killings, torture).
scoring_rationale.EQU: 2.0 confirmed. Indigenous, low-income, and prison populations bear disproportionate harm; floor-proximate developing.
scoring_rationale.BND: 2.5 confirmed under pressure. Internal armed conflict declaration, military deployment for civilian-policing scope, arbitrary arrest pattern, prison-system criminal-governance — boundary erosion documented but constitutional framework still functioning.
scoring_rationale.ACC: 2.5 confirmed under pressure. Defensoría del Pueblo / Constitutional Court functional; security-state operationalization compromises accountability mechanisms.
scoring_rationale.SYS: 2.5 confirmed under pressure. Carceral-capture documentation indicates structural state-capacity gap.
scoring_rationale.INT: 2.5 confirmed under pressure. Declared rights commitments contradicted by Fénix-plan operational pattern.

math_check.validator_check: Reconstruction matches published 35.9 exactly. No math-hygiene flag.

negativeSignals: Fénix plan rights erosion; arbitrary arrests; extrajudicial killings; detainee torture; prison-system carceral capture; among most violent LAm countries; sustained military deployment for civilian policing.
positiveSignals: Functioning Defensoría del Pueblo and Constitutional Court; civil society monitoring access; 2023 institutional crisis (Lasso dissolution / Noboa election) handled within constitutional framework; international monitoring access (Global Initiative, Freedom House).
sector_context: Latin America compound cluster with Argentina (labor reform) and Colombia (HRD killings + OHCHR urgent action). Ecuador distinguished by security-state crackdown (Fénix) and prison-system carceral-capture pattern.
open_actions_resolved: Freedom House 2026 + Global Initiative January 2026 absorbed as in-window evidence
open_actions_carried: 60-day re-evaluation: Fénix plan operationalization and any structural torture / extrajudicial-killing case proceedings
Watch: Constitutional Court rulings on internal-armed-conflict declaration
Watch: prison-system reform initiatives and any reduction in carceral-capture indicators
